message.content:
@startuml
participant "User Agent" as UA
participant "Content removal API" as API
participant "User Manager" as UM
participant "User database" as UD
participant "Content database" as CD

note right of UA
Should also include a CSRF token to prevent admins who are also visit other, potentially XSS vulnerable or malicious sites from helping an unknown actor remove content without authorization.
@endnote

UA -> API: Removal request (HTTP POST), contains ID of content to be removed.

API -> UM: Retrieve role of requesting user.
UM -> UD: Fetch user information from database.
UM -> API: Return query result.
API -> UM: Return user role.

alt [User has admin role]
    API -> API: Validate removal request
    alt [Failed to validate removal request]
        API -> API: Return an HTTP status code that signals validation failure.
        API -> API: Exact value depends on how the validation failed.
    end
    alt [Request is deemed valid]
        API -> CD: Remove content from DB.
        API -> CD: Result of delete operation
    end
    alt [Content has been removed successfully]
        API -> API: Return HTTP 204 No Content
    end
    alt [Removal failed]
        API -> API: Return HTTP Status code to signal reason for failure.
    end
    alt [User is not authorized to remove content]
        API -> API: Return HTTP 403 Forbidden to signal that the user has no permission to request what they just did.
    end
end

@enduml
